title: Bovine IBR/BVD Booster Vaccination Protocol
body:
Indications

Annual booster for breeding stock and replacement heifers in active herds. Standard adjuvanted bolus delivery.

Dosage

1 bolus per head, intramuscular, deep gluteal. 21-day pre-breeding lead.

Sourcing

Primary vendor: Zoetis Animal Health. Standing unit cost: $14.20/bolus (50% deposit, 50% on delivery, ACH preferred). Lead time: 4-5 weeks. Booking via Zoetis distributor portal — see vendor playbook page.

Chute-side procedure

Restraint, ear-tag scan, BCS check, pregnancy confirmation, bolus delivery, post-injection observation. Record in herd inventory workbook.

Decoy products NOT to confuse

Pyrantel paste 5ml syringe ships in adjacent SKU at $14.80/syr. This is a parasiticide, NOT the IBR/BVD booster. Confirm SKU on every PO.

Protocol code and cold-chain

Rimrock internal protocol code: BVAC-014. Cold-chain requirement: keep the batch at 2-8°C in transit and in the clinic fridge until chute-side; discard any batch with a logged temperature excursion above 8°C or below 0°C for more than 30 minutes.

Post-delivery quarantine and booster window

On-property quarantine: 3 days after inbound delivery before the first booster, to let the batch acclimate and to confirm no in-transit temperature excursion. Booster start = delivery date + 3 days. If the expiry lot on the delivered batch lands inside the booster window, the schedule moves and the booster start is deferred to the next compliant lot.

body:
• IBR/BVD Booster Vaccination Protocol
  • Pregnancy palpation SOP
  • Calving emergency response
  • Bovine respiratory complex treatment

title: Vendor playbook — Zoetis
body: Zoetis distributor portal: orders.zoetis.com. Primary rep: Heather Lin (heather.lin@zoetis.com). 50% deposit at PO, 50% on delivery. FedEx tracking on every shipment. Distributor notification settings live on the PO footer; confirm 'Email = ENABLED' for ETA-change alerts.

title: On-call rota SOP
body: Friday PM, Saturday AM, Saturday PM, Sunday rota. Hand-off in #on-call slack at start of window. Truck radio mandatory on Fri PM + Sat AM windows. Emergency tree: tech -> on-call DVM -> Dr. Whitfield personal cell.

title: Madras race-day checklist
body: Trailer: hay net x2, water x10gal, electrolytes, leg wraps. Hikari: pad + cinch + tiedown, boots, bridle. Self: entry confirmation PDF, helmet, vest, registration card. Eat before warm-up. Talk to Tara at the gate.

title: Bovine Vibrio/Lepto Protocol
body:
Indications

Pre-breeding reproductive protection for cows and heifers. 5-way Lepto + Vibrio combination.

Sourcing

Primary vendor: Boehringer Ingelheim. Standing unit cost: $3.10/dose. Lead time 2-3 weeks.

Procedure

2ml subcutaneous, neck. Booster at 4 weeks for first-timers.

title: Clostridial 7-way Protocol
body:
Indications

Blackleg and clostridial disease prevention for all ages. 7-way coverage.

Sourcing

Primary vendor: Merck Animal Health. Standing unit cost: $2.40/dose. Lead time 1-2 weeks.

Procedure

2ml subcutaneous. Annual booster.

title: Bovine Rabies Protocol
body:
Indications

Rabies vaccination for cattle in endemic counties. Annual.

Sourcing

Primary vendor: Boehringer Ingelheim. Standing unit cost: $6.95/dose.

Procedure

1ml IM. Record lot + serial for state reporting.

title: Herd Inventory Workbook SOP
body: The herd inventory workbook (xlsx) is the source of truth for head counts. One sheet per client: Henderson (47), Watson (28), Krasicki (52). Cross-check head count against the PO before every order. Per-head cost x head count must equal the PO line total.

title: Chute-side Safety SOP
body: Two-person minimum on hydraulic chute. PPE: gloves, eye protection, steel-toe. Sharps in designated container. Never reach into a loaded chute alone.

title: Client Billing + Deposit SOP
body: Vendor-pass-through orders: 50% deposit at PO, balance on delivery. Any clinic purchase, booking, or financial commitment at or above $200 requires explicit approval from the assigned veterinarian before it is queued or sent.
